reasons: Atlas creates a grouped teamfight setup that gives Zetian a clear AoE follow-up window.
Zetian can add magic pressure and global control timing after Atlas pulls enemies into one area.

proof.summary: Atlas creates a grouped teamfight setup, while Zetian follows with AoE control and magic damage.
worksBestWhen: Zetian is close enough to follow the pull.
The team saves damage for the grouped-control window.
failureCases: Atlas catches only a frontline tank.
Zetian is too far away to follow.
Enemy disengage breaks the setup before Zetian commits.

reasons: Tigreal can pull and stun nearby enemies, giving Zetian a clean AoE follow-up window.
Tigreal also provides frontline presence so Zetian can cast from safer spacing.

proof.summary: Tigreal pulls and stuns nearby enemies, giving Zetian a clear AoE follow-up window.
worksBestWhen: Tigreal engages after Zetian is close enough to follow.
Tigreal catches multiple enemies, not only the frontline.
Zetian saves major cooldowns for the confirmed pull/stun.
failureCases: Tigreal's channel is interrupted before the pull completes.
Zetian is too far away to follow.
Enemy heroes spread before Tigreal finds an angle.

reasons: Carmilla links enemies so Zetian's AoE magic pressure and control can punish multiple targets together.
Zetian benefits when Carmilla starts the teamfight by connecting enemies before the main damage window.

proof.summary: Carmilla links enemies, while Zetian adds AoE magic pressure and control into the linked teamfight.
worksBestWhen: Carmilla links multiple enemies before Zetian commits major cooldowns.
Zetian casts into the linked group.
The team follows quickly before the link value ends.
failureCases: Zetian uses key skills before Carmilla confirms the link.
Enemies spread or disengage before shared damage and control matter.

reasons: Chip converts Zetian's global reveal and control window into actual team arrival.
Zetian can expose enemy positions, while Chip gives the team a direct way to collapse on the chosen target.

proof.summary: Zetian reveals and briefly controls enemies globally, while Chip turns that window into a portal collapse.
worksBestWhen: Zetian casts ultimate before Chip chooses the Shortcut target.
Chip targets an isolated or objective-side enemy.
failureCases: Zetian's global window ends before teammates arrive.
The team enters one by one and gets punished.

reasons: Yi Sun-shin adds global reveal from a different role and damage profile than Zetian.
Zetian can use Yi Sun-shin's reveal to choose whether to commit her global control window for collapse or objective control.

proof.summary: Yi Sun-shin reveals enemy positions first, then Zetian can choose whether to commit her global stun and movement window for collapse or objective control.
worksBestWhen: Yi Sun-shin reveals before Lord, Turtle, or jungle invade timing.
Zetian saves ultimate until the team identifies the best target or enemy formation.
The team has enough engage or mobility to act on the information.
failureCases: Both global skills are used at the same time without a follow-up plan.
The team lacks engage after the reveal.
Enemy heroes group safely and avoid isolated rotations.
